items.title: Zero-visibility swamp dive uncovers water pipeline failure in major city

children.content: A decades-old pipeline supplying water to nearly 1 million people in Papua New Guinea's capital is at risk of collapsing without urgent repairs, threatening to cut off more than half of the city's water supply.

gallery.altText: A group of men are seen standing in a swamp, while a diver's head emerges from a deep pool of brown water.

thumbnail.altText: A group of men are seen standing in a swamp, while a diver's head emerges from a deep pool of brown water.

items.title: Self-managed super sector blindsided by Labor-Greens deal on tax package

children.content: A ban on self-managed super funds borrowing money to buy housing has blindsided the sector, which says Labor should instead target "unscrupulous" operators spruiking property as an investment.

image.altText: Two suited men walk towards lecterns.

items.title: Why your power bill may rise despite the lower default market offer

children.content: Some electricity users have been hit with higher charges, despite all the talk of the lower default market offers. Retailers say a rebalancing of supply and usage charges is the cause, but there are signs some are clawing back profit by raising their cheapest plans.

image.altText: A graphic image of a power plug overlaid on a house and electricity bill

items.title: 'Punched in the guts': Sacked academics take uni to Fair Work Commission

children.content: Two senior academics from Macquarie University allege they were targeted for redundancy because of their union activity.

image.altText: A middle aged woman with short white hair sitting in a loungeroom

items.title: UN inquiry accuses Israel of deliberately targeting and killing Palestinian children

children.content: The Israeli government is condemning the inquiry and rejecting its findings, labelling the United Nations report "deeply flawed" and full of "errors and distortions" designed to push a "politically-driven anti-Israel narrative".

image.altText: A young child looking through a makeshift tent's window.

images.altText: Cristiano Ronaldo holds up his finger

children.content: Cristiano Ronaldo scores twice as Portugal completes a 5-0 drubbing of Uzbekistan. Later, England meets Ghana in Boston. Follow live.

items.title: 'I'm back': Ronaldo makes a statement in five-star Portugal win

updates.title: Red card brings red mist as commentator loses credentials
FT: Portugal bounce back with 5-0 thumping of Uzbekistan

items.title: ASIC warns of 'extremely high' car loan fees amid rising complaints

children.content: A new report warns customers about traps they face when taking out a car loan, including high interest rates and fees.

image.altText: A woman with a leg injury sits in the front seat of her car holding the steering wheel.

items.title: Map to collect data to bridge gaps in suicide prevention, researchers say

children.content: Policymakers, mental health experts and those working on the front line will use the National Suicide Prevention Outcomes Map to gauge what services are working to prevent people from reaching critical crisis points.

image.altText: Jo Riley smiles while in a garden. She is wearing a colourful top and a necklace.

items.title: 'De-gayed for TV for 26 years': Gardening Australia star is proud to be out

children.content: Gardening Australia star Jerry Coleby-Williams hid his identity on TV for 26 years, but has never been ashamed of being a gay man.

gallery.altText: Jerry Coleby-Williams stands outside smiling, holding up a hanging pot overflowing with cherry tomatoes

thumbnail.altText: Jerry Coleby-Williams stands outside smiling, holding up a hanging pot overflowing with cherry tomatoes

items.title: Australians financially supporting family in Lebanon say they have no other option

children.content: Lebanese Australians, who have spent thousands of dollars to help their loved ones survive a two-and-a-half-year war, bear a financial burden that also comes with an emotional toll.

image.altText: A photo of Sue (Far right) with her family pictured behind them in Lebanon.

items.title: Senior Albanese minister clashes with Pocock over top-secret AI copyright claim

children.content: Federal politician David Pocock has clashed with a senior Albanese government minister over a whistleblower claim that federal cabinet is considering plans to let AI companies train on Australian copyright material.

image.altText: Pocock gestures with both hands, partially obscured by the silhouettes of press gallery journalists he is speaking to.

items.title: Animals 'disappearing' from the wild as more people seek exotic pets

children.content: Australian and international authorities are "struggling" to keep up with regulating and policing the exotic pet market as demand surges.

items.title: Daryl Braithwaite announces end to live performances

children.content: Australian vocalist, former Sherbet frontman and ARIA Hall of Fame inductee Daryl Braithwaite says he is stepping back from singing live.

image.altText: Daryl Braithwaite on ARIA Hall of Fame status and why he still loves performing

items.title: Dead birds to be tested for H5N1 bird flu after washing up on SA coast

children.content: Two dead seabirds have been collected and are being tested for the H5N1 bird flu virus in South Australia, as well as another two wild birds in Western Australia, following Australia's first confirmed cases of the deadly variant this week.

image.altText: A hand with a cloth holding the beak of a dead grey and white bird on a beach.

items.title: Cosy ways to keep warm this winter without turning the heater on

children.content: Heating your whole house — or even a room — can be expensive in the depths of winter. Keeping the humans inside it warm is often cheaper and more efficient.

gallery.altText: A close up of the hand of a person with black painted nails, wearing a cream wooly jumper, holding a cup of tea.

thumbnail.altText: A close up of the hand of a person with black painted nails, wearing a cream wooly jumper, holding a cup of tea.

items.title: Quiz: Join us for a mid-week trip around the world

children.content: Which town would you be in if you were sunbathing at Cable Beach? Where does Maldon sea salt come from?

items.title: 'Look at us go!': First Nations actors celebrate Logies nods

children.content: With strong representation of Indigenous talent in this year's Logie Award nominations, actor Lila McGuire says it's a "very important time" and "a gift for young mob seeing so many of us excelling".

image.altText: LIla as Evonne takes a swing at a tennise ball during a match.

items.title: 'We lost our son': India's medical exams scandal leads to tragedy and uproar

children.content: More than 2 million students across India have resat the country's highly competitive medical entrance exam after last month's test was cancelled amid allegations that questions had been leaked.

gallery.altText: Four women in floral veils have their heads bowed, holding a photo of a young man wearing glasses.

thumbnail.altText: Four women in floral veils have their heads bowed, holding a photo of a young man wearing glasses.

items.title: Australia approves restricted use of herbicide banned in 70 other countries

children.content: The Australian Pesticides and Veterinary Medicines Authority concludes its nearly 30-year review of the controversial but popular herbicide.

image.altText: A label on a plastic tub reads CORROSIVE LIQUID, TOXIC N.O.S. PARAQUAT DICHLORIDE

items.title: Court told alleged cult leader groomed girl to have 45 of his children

children.content: William "Little Pebble" Kamm has denied allegations he groomed a girl for more than a decade after he was accused of declaring in phone calls that it was her "mission" to become his queen.

image.altText: An old man with grey hair, bushy eyebrows and glasses walking towards a building.

items.title: Chihuahua Jet takes to the air as more pets allowed in cabin

children.content: An Australian airline is allowing small cats and dogs on a range of flights, with other passengers appearing unconcerned about sharing the plane with furry creatures.

gallery.altText: A woman holding a small dog in a carrier at an airport with a sign for Paw-riority boarding

thumbnail.altText: A woman holding a small dog in a carrier at an airport with a sign for Paw-riority boarding

items.title: Kids Helpline crisis interventions on the rise again towards COVID high

children.content: Kids Helpline says a growing number of Australian young people are requiring immediate emergency help, with a concerning trend around crisis interventions.

image.altText: Girl sitting down with her hands clasped.

items.title: 'They've been caught out': KPMG fallout continues with resignations

children.content: KPMG chair Martin Sheppard and two audit partners are leaving the accounting and consulting giant in the wake of a scandal over misuse of confidential client information and the alleged mistreatment of a whistleblower.

image.altText: KPMG

items.title: Newly discovered spider uses 'ballista' web to capture prey

children.content: A bizarre species of spider from the rainforests of Far North Queensland springs a nasty surprise.

items.title: Suspended Qld police officer facing an array of charges granted bail

children.content: A suspended Queensland police officer arrested over alleged offences against multiple women has been granted bail.

image.altText: The Queensland Police Service logo and insignia seen at a press conference on April 6, 2025.

items.title: Video of nurse allegedly threatening Israelis excluded from court case

children.content: Lawyers argued the viral video clip from an online chat with an Israeli influencer breached NSW laws prohibiting recording private conversations without consent.

image.altText: Ahmad Rashad Nadir and Sarah Abu Lebdeh both wearing sunglasses.

items.title: Perfect dupes of ABC articles are fuelling an industrial-scale scam

children.content: A $350 million scam is targeting Australians using AI-generated images of ABC journalists and politicians.

items.title: Union passes 'no confidence' vote against SA's top cop and deputy

children.content: South Australia's police union passes a vote of no confidence in the leadership of the police commissioner and his deputy, despite the top cop saying the move was "not reflective" of the feedback he received from officers.

items.title: Church youth leader sentenced for sexually assaulting 13-year-old

children.content: A man who assaulted a girl in his car has dodged a lengthy jail term, with a judge saying there were "exceptional circumstances" that warranted a discounted sentence.

image.altText: Joshua Emonson poses for a photo with four other people

items.title: 'Ridiculous and disrespectful': Inquiry into NDIS cuts extended

children.content: Disability advocates welcome an extension to a Senate inquiry probing a bill paving the way for the biggest-ever cuts to the NDIS.

items.title: Award-winning artwork revealed as 'blatant copy'

children.content: The Doyles award committee is investigating why it took nearly 12 months to discover a winning landscape painting was an imitation of another artist's work.

image.altText: A photo of an artwork depicting two childlike figures at the beach.

items.title: Meet the 'King of the North' set to become the UK's new PM

children.content: A popular mayor but a virtual unknown on the global stage, Andy Burnham is poised to become Sir Keir Starmer's successor. Here's what that could look like.

articles.title: 'They've lost the plot': UK Labour could learn from Rudd-Gillard soap opera

children.content: After the era of Rudd-Gillard-Rudd, the ALP said: "never again." Sir Keir Starmer's resignation on Monday means we're about to find out if Britain's Labour MPs were watching.

image.altText: Ken Rudd, wearing a dark suit, gestures with his right hand while holding a pen. Julia Gillard is in the background.

avatar.alt: A man smiling at the camera.

articles.title: More or Less quiz: Which British PMs have lasted the longest?

children.content: Some British PMs have held office across decades. Others have struggled to outlast a lettuce. It's time to test your knowledge.

image.altText: A screenshot from a Daily Star broadcast shows a lettuce with googly eyes next to portrait of Liz Truss

articles.title: Andy Burnham sworn in as MP in hours after Starmer's resignation

children.content: Sir Keir Starmer says he will resign as prime minister of the United Kingdom and spend more time as a husband and father.
